title: #product: 6378901234
preview:
Priya Desai: Quick sync — we have a customer asking for an end-to-end pilot of FastRoute in ~2 weeks. Main blockers: infra prewarm (reserved capacity), runtime quantization/profile, and KMS/HSM key flow. I think we should decide staged scope today. TL;DR: staging-only pilot first, HSM later. Thoughts?
Alex Chen: Staging-only sounds safest. Runtime can cut a quantization profile and a conservative batching default, but 2 weeks is tight. Can we limit telemetry and non-blocking features? :thinking_face:
Samir Patel: Capacity note: to guarantee latency SLOs we need to reserve a small GPU pool and prewarm it for the pilot window. That requires a capacity request and cost sign-off. 2 weeks -> we need the request by EOD to make it.
Jordan Blake: Security: HSM/KMS review + customer key ownership is non-trivial. We can run without HSM in staging if the customer accepts envelope keys, but production (or anything with BYOK) needs the KMS review and attestation.
Riley Gomez: Customer preference is end-to-end with keys, but they said they'd accept staging-only to validate latency and UX. Product priority is verifying p95 latency and core correctness, not BYOK yet.
Alex Chen: Proposed rollout plan (small):
```
rollout:
  stage1: staging-only (no HSM), canary 10% internal, reduced telemetry
  stage2: HSM-enabled (BYOK) after KMS review, full telemetry
runtime_configs:
  default_batch: 32
  model_variant: quantized-v2
  fallback: fp16-v1
```
Samir Patel: Ok — if we do staging-only, infra needs a pinned pool for the pilot window and a jittered prewarm schedule. I'll file the capacity request. @Priya please confirm expected pilot traffic shape (qps, concurrency).
Priya Desai: Expected pilot traffic: ~150 qps peak, concurrency spike to ~60. We'll provide a small synthetic trace. Priorities: p95 < 250ms, correct fallbacks.
Jordan Blake: I'll kick off the KMS/HSM intake with security docs but it will slip past the 2-week pilot unless we deprioritize other reviews.
Alex Chen: For rollout safety, add this fallback rule: `if gpu_latency > 500ms -> route to fp16-v1`. Also enable KV prefix caching for common prompts. That helps p95.
deploy-bot: scheduled prewarm job created: `prewarm-fastroute-637890` -> starts in 36h (automated).
Riley Gomez: Decision call: staging-only pilot in 2w with reserved capacity + reduced telemetry. HSM/BYOK moved to next cycle (Q3). Action items: @Samir file capacity request by EOD, @Jordan start KMS intake, @Alex ship quant profile + fallback, @Priya send traffic trace. Agree? :thumbsup:
Samir Patel: Filing capacity request now.
Jordan Blake: Starting KMS intake, will post checklist.
Alex Chen: I'll drop quant profile PR today and add the fallback rule.
Priya Desai: Sending traffic trace in #product-docs in 15m.
Riley Gomez: Great — locking staging-first. We can reconvene mid-week if anything blocks. Thanks everyone. :rocket:

title: #product: 6151234567
preview:
Asha Reddy: Quick pulse 99	we need a compact DP run to validate Private onboarding and our replay harness. Goal: 6 partners, 3-week staggered starts, 21-45 day pilots depending on complexity.

Ben Carter: +1. Keep screener minimal: use case, compliance (HIPAA/SOC2/PCI), expected QPS.

Ivy Cho: SLOs: gen p50 < 80ms, p95 < 300ms @256 tokens. embeddings p50 < 30ms, p95 < 80ms. Separate buckets.

Samir Khan: Success gates: 1) E2E integration + smoke tests 2) perf SLOs met in replay run 3) 6 labeled failure cases submitted 4) partner agrees to feedback cadence.

Asha Reddy: Incentives: 2 months discounted Dedicated credits + up to 3 engineering days. Offer co-marketing for strategic partners.

Ivy Cho: Tooling we'll provide: replay harness (private repo), token sensor script, eval CSV template. Partners must run smoke tests in staging for 72h.

Ben Carter: Legal needs anonymized metrics consent and a light DP addendum. Ops will allow limited SLA windows during pilot.

dp-bot: COHORT CREATED dp-priv-mini-2025-11. Invitations queued for 6 prospects. Kickoffs tentatively week of 2025-11-17.

Asha Reddy: Action items: @Ben finalize DP addendum; @Ivy publish eval template and smoke script; @Samir prep replay how-to. I'll shortlist prospects and post invites by EOD Friday.

Samir Khan: I'll prepare the replay doc and post to #product/tools by Thu.

Ivy Cho: Eval template ready, sharing link in outreach.

Maria Gonzales: runtime-v3 autoscale — shipped ✅ Canary at 50% for web tier. No major regressions so far. PR: https://github.com/redwood/runtime/pull/421 needs review (mainly infra test changes). Next: roll to 100% after Chen reviews.

Chen Li: I'll review PR421 this afternoon. Heads-up: one e2e test flakes on Dedicated env — that's the slipped bit. Blocking dep: eng-infra needs the new GPU image (ticket #PLAT-87). Next-step: fix flake + coordinate image bump with infra.

Samir Patel: console telemetry retention feature — slipped. Storage budget exceeded during load test. Short-term mitigation: retention toggle + backfill compaction job. PR: https://github.com/redwood/console/pull/88 (needs Jordan). Owner: Samir. ETA: patch and canary next Tue.

Jordan Blake: Picking up the console PR review now. Also raising infra quota symlink ticket — pinged eng-infra. If infra gives quota, can land in patch window.

deploy-bot: Scheduled canary deploy for runtime-v3 autoscale at 2026-03-22T18:00Z. Monitoring channels: #eng-oncall, #eng-runtime.

Maria Gonzales: Quick note — model-routing canary completed on EU region: no perf regressions. Shipped ✅

Chen Li: Dependency map: runtime-v3 autoscale -> infra image PLAT-87 -> quota (infra) -> network policy (security). I'll add this to the OKR doc.

Samir Patel: For PR reviewers: if you can pick a PR today, add :eyes: reaction. My PR88 needs one more approver from product or observability.

title: #product: 5567890123
preview:
Nina Park: Heads up 1: we have two regulated pilot customers asking for SLA exceptions (longer data residency commitments + legal indemnities). Need a quick alignment: what parts of GA gating do we relax vs what must stay for public GA? @Raj @Omar @Lena @Ben @Emma

Raj Singh: Short answer: legal can approve limited SLA exceptions for named accounts but only if security controls (VPC, KMS) and audit logs are in place. We need clear scope and a time-limited clause (e.g., 6 months) and commercial signoff. Also require change to TOS addendum.

Omar Reyes: Security checklist for exceptions: 1) Dedicated or Private deployment, 2) Customer-managed KMS, 3) Audit log retention >= 1 year, 4) Pen-test within 90 days, 5) Network allowlist. If any of these are missing, we cannot relax data residency.

Lena Mfcller: Sales note: both customers are willing to sign a pilot addendum and pay for extra support. They want SLA credits higher than standard (e.g., 99.99 for Dedicated). We should capture an explicit billing backstop if we agree to tighter SLA.

Ben Carter: Operationally we can support 99.99 for small committed Dedicated pools but it requires reserved capacity + on-call increase during pilot. We should model cost: estimate +30% on ops headcount for the pilot period. Also need a fallback policy if infra risk appears (auto-degrade to 99.95 and notify).

Emma Zhao: Finance: we'll need a billing backstop clause that limits credits (max X months) and ties credits to measured downtime only. Also need telemetry to validate $/token and effective cost delta for Dedicated vs Hosted. I can fast-track a simple P&L if Ben shares ops delta.

DeployBot: NOTE: security controls doc template: https://internal.docs/redwood/security-exception-template (created).

Nina Park: Proposed path: 1) Draft exception addendum (Raj) by Wed, 2) Security gap check (Omar + Ben) Tue, 3) Draft billing backstop terms (Emma) Wed, 4) Sales finalize customer addendum (Lena) Thu. Pilot starts only after checklist cleared. Any objections?

Raj Singh: No objections. Will include TOS change + 6-month sunset + liability caps.

Omar Reyes: Will run gap check Tue morning and upload findings to the shared doc.

Ben Carter: Will model ops cost and a fallback automation by Tue EOD.

Emma Zhao: Will prepare billing backstop language and a short financial model by Wed.

Lena Mfcller: Fine by me. I'll align customers and use the addendum template.

Nina Park: Great. Quick ask: if we accept these exceptions for pilot, do we mark feature as "GA with exceptions" on the product page or keep it as "Pilot/Private only" until we generalize? Thoughts?

Raj Singh: Keep public GA as-is. Exceptions should be private/pilot-only and documented in the addendum.

Ben Carter: Agreed. Also add explicit rollback triggers (incidents > X in 30 days -> reevaluate SLA).

Nina Park: Thanks all. I'll compile notes and update the pre-GA checklist with an "Exceptions" section. Will post updated checklist here once done. :white_check_mark:

title: #product: 5220001111
preview:
Evan Park: Quick thread 99need alignment on two catalog items for next sprint: (1) 'cerberus-lite-1' onboarding as a fast-path small model, (2) staged deprecation timeline for 'falcon-old-v2' pinned customers. High level proposal in the next message.
Leah Morgan: +1 let's capture launch gates early.
Diego Ruiz: Curious about target customers for cerberus-lite-1. Is this for low-latency mobile use cases or just cost-sensitive tenants?
Priya Natarajan: Primary use case = latency-sensitive mobile + cheap embedding fallback. We should classify as "tier B" model with opt-in pin.
Ben Cho: Ops note: cerberus-lite-1 requires different quantization runtime. Need infra sign-off. Can reserve a 1-GPU test pool.
Evan Park: Proposal bullets:
- Cerberus-lite-1 onboarding: 7-day quick canary (hosted only), bench targets: p95 <= 90ms, regressions <=3% vs baseline on mobile prompts, token-cost <= -12% (cheaper). Gate: perf harness green + infra smoke.
- Falcon-old-v2 deprecation: 3 cohort rollout: internal -> partner opt-in -> pinned customers with 45d opt-out -> forced retire at 90d. Provide a migration credit program for impacted customers for 60 days.
Leah Morgan: Migration credit = how many free tokens? we should propose a $/month credit estimate rather than tokens. Customers understand dollars.
Priya Natarajan: Agree. Suggest credit = 20% of monthly estimated token spend or flat $300/mo whichever is higher, for customers on pinned contracts.
Diego Ruiz: We must run compatibility checks for prompt schema differences; add an automated smoke that compares top-10 customer prompt outputs and flags >7% semantic change.
Ben Cho: I'll spin up the 1-GPU pool and run a quick quant-tune. ETA: Tue morning.
Evan Park: Acceptance criteria for cerberus-lite-1 (short):
- perf harness: p95 <= 90ms
- quality pass >= 97% on eval_mobile_v2
- infra smoke: no OOMs at 95th percentile concurrent load
- docs: quickstart + known-diff section published
Leah Morgan: For deprecation comms: we need a staged email + console banner + in-product modal for pinned users. Drafting copy now.
Priya Natarajan: Legal check: migration credit formula in contract addendum. I'll ping legal.
Diego Ruiz: Can we add a "compat shim" flag to route to falcon-old-v2 fallback for 30 days post-migrate? That reduces customer risk.
Ben Cho: Technically feasible. Will need routing config and cost cap guardrails.
comm-bot: Ticket created: catalog-cerberus-lite-5220001111 (status: open), ticket: falcon-old-v2-deprec-5220001111 (status: drafting).
Evan Park: Decision asks by Wed EOD: (A) greenlight cerberus-lite-1 quick canary with the listed gates, (B) approve deprecation cohorts + migration credit formula. Please reply with approve/need-changes.
Leah Morgan: Approve (A) pending perf results. Need to iterate comm copy for (B) before final approve.
Priya Natarajan: Approve (B) in principle, pending legal on contract language.
Diego Ruiz: Approve (A) and (B) if compat shim routing is in place.
Ben Cho: Approve (A) once infra smoke completes. I'll report back Tue.
Evan Park: Thanks all 99I'll lock these into the sprint if no objections by Wed EOD.

title: #product: 2310001234
preview:
Alex Kim: Heads-up — ACME pilot asked explicitly for the same response formats as Competitor X (CSV + per-field confidence). They're pushing for parity on output schema. We need to decide whether to scope a parity workstream for GA or defer to a later add-on.
Priya Rao: Is this blocking the pilot launch or just a nice-to-have?
Alex Kim: Pilot is green for a narrow MVP, but ACME says their integration tests fail without the CSV export. They could delay if we can't ship a small adapter.
Ben Carter: Quick question: can we hack an export adapter in Console > Exports with 2 engineer-weeks?
Marta Lopez: We could, but we should check productization cost (support, docs, testing). If it's a one-off adapter, ops burden might be high.
Priya Rao: Proposed options: 
1) Ship an adapter in Dedicated as a short-term patch (P0 for pilot).
2) Add a generic output-shaping feature to Redwood API (P1 for GA, longer timeline).
3) Ask ACME to run a thin translation layer on their side.
Ben Carter: Rough estimates: adapter = 1.5w eng + 0.5w QA + 0.5w docs. Generic output shaping = 4w eng + 2w QA + extra design.
Alex Kim: Tradeoffs: adapter -> faster but technical debt. Generic feature -> durable but delays pilot. Asking ACME -> lowest engineering cost but risks customer experience and sales momentum.
Marta Lopez: We should evaluate strategic value beyond ACME. If multiple enterprise prospects are asking for schema-level parity, generic feature might be reusable. Anyone seen other asks?
Priya Rao: Sales noted 3 other deals asking for CSV/field-level confidence, but not all are blockers. Counts as medium signal.
Ben Carter: Suggest a landing-zone: ship adapter for pilot + scope a generic output-shaping RFC for Q2 if we see repeat demand. That buys time while keeping product direction.
Alex Kim: I like that. Also need clear launch criteria for the adapter: 
- Export format mapping file committed
- End-to-end integration test with ACME sample payloads
- 1 rollback plan (fallback endpoint)
- Support KB + minimal Console UI to enable/disable adapter
Marta Lopez: Add SLA note: adapter will not be covered under Dedicated latency SLA until we complete robustness test (1 week stabilization). Communicate to sales.
Priya Rao: For prioritization, here's a tiny matrix I made: 
```
Item,Impact,Effort,Recommended
Adapter for ACME,High,Low,P0
Generic output-shaping,Medium,High,P1
Ask ACME to translate,Low,Negligible,P2
```
Ben Carter: OK. Can eng-oncall own the rollout monitoring for the first 72 hours? @eng-bot can we create a temporary alert for adapter errors > 1%?
eng-bot: created alert "adapter_errors_pct" threshold 1% -> notify #eng-oncall, created at 2310001400
Alex Kim: Next: docs + API spec change log. Priya, can you draft the contributor-facing RFC for the generic work while we implement the adapter? Aim for a short doc by EOD tomorrow.
Priya Rao: Yep. I'll include acceptance criteria and sample mapping schema.
Marta Lopez: Sales comms: I'll draft a one-pager explaining the adapter is a pilot-only convenience and that GA will consolidate via API features (so we don't promise long-term parity for free).
Ben Carter: One more thing — can we re-use the existing export pipeline? If yes, dev time drops. I'll spike today.
Alex Kim: Agreement: ship adapter (P0) for ACME pilot, scope generic feature as P1 RFC, monitoring & rollback plan in place. We'll reassess after two weeks of usage.
Priya Rao: Noted. I'll make a checklist card in the product board and tag stakeholders.
Marta Lopez: Thanks everyone :thumbsup:
Ben Carter: I'll post a short status update in #sales-readout once I know if spike is a win.
Alex Kim: Closing thread — tagging: @Priya Rao @Ben Carter @Marta Lopez @eng-bot.

Priya Rao: Proposed options: 
1) Ship an adapter in Dedicated as a short-term patch (P0 for pilot).
2) Add a generic output-shaping feature to Redwood API (P1 for GA, longer timeline).
3) Ask ACME to run a thin translation layer on their side.
Ben Carter: Rough estimates: adapter = 1.5w eng + 0.5w QA + 0.5w docs. Generic output shaping = 4w eng + 2w QA + extra design.
Alex Kim: Tradeoffs: adapter -> faster but technical debt. Generic feature -> durable but delays pilot. Asking ACME -> lowest engineering cost but risks customer experience and sales momentum.
Marta Lopez: We should evaluate strategic value beyond ACME. If multiple enterprise prospects are asking for schema-level parity, generic feature might be reusable. Anyone seen other asks?
Priya Rao: Sales noted 3 other deals asking for CSV/field-level confidence, but not all are blockers. Counts as medium signal.
Ben Carter: Suggest a landing-zone: ship adapter for pilot + scope a generic output-shaping RFC for Q2 if we see repeat demand. That buys time while keeping product direction.

- End-to-end integration test with ACME sample payloads

Item,Impact,Effort,Recommended

Generic output-shaping,Medium,High,P1

preview:
Ava Chen: Hey product/design — quick review request for the autoscale onboarding flow (SLO slider + min/max + burst fallback). Noticed a couple of clunky spots, can we microcopy + a11y sweep?

Sofi Gomez: Accessibility note: pool picker traps focus when you open it and then close — VoiceOver reads duplicate headings. Tab order needs to be linear.
Marco Rivera: Copy thought on "burst": users confused if it's permanent. Suggest rename to "Burst capacity (temporary)" or add a short helper: "Extra GPUs for short spikes — billed separately".
Ellie Tran: UX concern on fallback: when selected pool is exhausted we fallback to hosted model. We need the toast copy to be explicit about data routing & perf. Current toast: "Using fallback pool" is too vague.
Ava Chen: Good calls. For slider defaults maybe we set a soft default at 70% with a subtle tooltip: "Start conservative — we’ll suggest higher based on traffic." Thoughts? :thinking_face: 
Liam Patel: 70% seems safer. Also suggest we show immediate cost delta next to slider (small grey text) so SREs understand dollar impact.
Sofi Gomez: For keyboard: make pool picker a dialog role with aria-modal=true and add `aria-describedby` pointing to the helper copy. Also label the slider with `aria-valuetext` that reads e.g. "Reliability 70 percent — higher reliability may increase cost".
Marco Rivera: Microcopy proposal for burst helper: "Temporary burst capacity: short-term extra GPUs to absorb spikes. Automatically scales down after 10m." Could be shorter.

```
Fallback active: selected GPU pool at capacity. Redirecting requests to Redwood Hosted for availability. Data remains encrypted in transit; choose a Dedicated pool to avoid routing. (View details)
```

Liam Patel: Shorten to: "Lower cost ← → Lower latency & higher reliability" (with icons).
Sofi Gomez: Accessibility for that condensed copy: ensure it's still read by screen readers as full phrase using visually-hidden text.
Marco Rivera: UX edgecase: if user sets min=0 and burst enabled, what do we show for expected baseline capacity? Maybe show a tiny predicted throughput line chart sample so they understand cold-starts.

title: #product: 2141590000
preview:
Lina Torres: Quick sync—drafting the pilot outreach and acceptance criteria for our Private preview. Need decisions on gating, incentives, and what success looks like.
Ethan Park: Prefer we lock minimal security gates first (KMS or equivalent + documented network policy). Anything less and infra teams will push back.
Noah Reed: Agreed. Also suggest a lightweight compliance checklist: data residency, DPA required, and whether partner needs air-gapped install.
Asha Rao: For outreach messaging, we should highlight influence on product roadmap and concierge onboarding to increase conversion.
Lina Torres: Proposed gating checklist (working):
- Security: KMS/HSM or managed key policy in place
- Network: VPC peering or documented firewall rules
- Legal: pilot NDA & DPA baseline approved
- Ops: contact point for infra + rough QPS estimate
Ethan Park: Can we add a "minimum readiness" item: partner can allocate a technical lead for the pilot (8 hrs/wk)?
Noah Reed: That's fair. But don't hard-require reserved capacity; offer it as a paid upgrade for validation partners.
Asha Rao: Recruitment tiers idea:
- Exploratory partners: no commitment, quick feedback loop
- Validation partners: short reserved discount + SLA discussion
Lina Torres: Good. Incentives list to include in outreach:
1) Priority product feedback & roadmap session
2) Discounted reserved pricing for first 3 months
3) Dedicated onboarding window and playbook
4) Early access to new monitoring features
Ethan Park: Also promise a post-pilot review with performance metrics and a short exec summary. Partners love that.
Noah Reed: Feedback cadence suggestion: weekly async updates (shared doc) + 2 live meetings (kickoff + mid-pilot check + wrap). Collect: prompts/eval packs, latency/cost reports, integration notes.
Asha Rao: Success metrics we should track:
- Integration status (green/yellow/red)
- P95 latency vs agreed SLO
- Quality regression vs baseline (<5% default)
- Partner satisfaction (quick survey after milestones)
- Time-to-first-inference (TTFI)
Lina Torres: Let's pin initial default targets: p95 < 350ms for gen flows, quality regression < 5%, TTFI < 48 hours for standard setups. These can be tuned per partner.
Ethan Park: Cohort sizing: 3
5 partners max for first run. Prioritize partners with similar infra footprints to our target personas.
Noah Reed: Outreach channels: AEs, account owners, targeted community post, and referrals from existing Dedicated customers. Keep ask form short.
Asha Rao: Form fields: company, contact, infra type, expected qps, compliance needs, pilot goals, preferred start window, commitment tier.
Lina Torres: I'll draft the Typeform copy. Ethan—can you set up a simple spreadsheet for pipeline and status?
Ethan Park: Yep. I'll spin up the sheet and a Trello column for onboarding tasks. recruit-bot: created form -> https://example.typeform.com/redwood-private-preview (auto-post)
Noah Reed: Scope control: pilot scope = onboarding + one primary integration scenario. New feature requests get triaged into backlog and only high-impact ones get fast-tracked with partner consent. Use a change request template.
Asha Rao: Change request template should include impact, owner, timeline, and whether it blocks pilot success. If >2 scope additions then classify as extended work.
Lina Torres: Timeline: open invites next Mon, close in 10 business days, kickoff 3 weeks after close. Need final sign-off on gating and incentives by EOD today.
Ethan Park: I'm good with that. :thumbsup:
Noah Reed: +1
Asha Rao: +1
Lina Torres: Great. I'll push the outreach copy to #sales and post the acceptance form link. Thanks everyone!

title: #product: 2078901234
preview:
Ava Chen: Quick intake — customer (FinSage) ran a canary and saw weird regressions when we routed to quantized 8-bit variants. Problem statement: some tenants observed answer hallucinations and formatting drops only on the canary cohort. Need to capture goals, non-goals, and concrete success metrics for a PRD so infra/product can scope rollout controls. @Samir Shah @Priya Nair can you jump in? TL;DR proposal: per-tenant canary + auto-rollback on eval regressions + manual override in console.

Samir Shah: Thanks—quick Q: do we have a repro prompt set from the customer or is this flaky across many prompts? Also what was the traffic % for the canary?

Ava Chen: They shared ~120 failure examples but it's noisy. Canary was 5% traffic for 2h, and errors spiked after a model compact/quantize push. We should treat this as a plausible production regression vector.

Priya Nair: Proposed goals / non-goals (first pass):
- Goals: detect quality regressions per-tenant during rollout, auto-rollback if regression exceeds threshold, surface clear root-cause (model variant + quant config), minimal user intervention for obvious regressions.
- Non-goals: deterministic model debugging (we won't attempt retraining), global-wide rollback policies (this is per-tenant first).
- Success metrics: detection precision >= 95% on labeled regression set; false positive rollback rate < 1% per week; mean rollback time < 45s; customer SLA impact <= 0.5% latency increase.

Liam O'Connor: A couple of edge cases worth listing:
- Intermittent regressions that correlate with specific prompt lengths (need sequence-bucketed evals).
- Tenant-level small sample sizes (5% traffic * small tenant => noisy stats). Mitigation: require sustained drop over window or combine with offline evaluation.

Priya Nair: Example acceptance criteria snippet (for the PRD):
```
AC-1: For any tenant-grade canary, if model variant shows >=3% absolute drop in quality score on the tenant's eval-suite sustained for 3 consecutive minutes, system triggers auto-rollback.
AC-2: Rollback should complete and route traffic back within <= 60s, and emit a rollback event with diagnostics (delta metrics, sample diffs, model id).
AC-3: Manual override button in Console must be available to operators and preserve audit logs.
```

Ava Chen: Nice. Can we tighten the rollback latency target to <=30s if infra says it's feasible? We need that for high-frequency services. @Samir any infra constraints?

Samir Shah: 30s is ambitious but doable if we prioritize the rollback path: pre-warm fallback replicas, keep kv cache primed, and avoid full cold-starts. We need autoscale playbook updates. Also implementing tenant-aware evaluation hooks will need a small infra spike (~2 sprint effort).

Liam O'Connor: Metrics specifics I'd add:
- Primary quality metric: per-tenant EVAL-PASS-RATE (binary per prompt) aggregated as rolling 1-min and 5-min windows.
- Alerting: trigger when 1-min drop >3% AND 5-min drop >1.5%.
- Diagnostic payload: sample diffs (orig vs variant), latency histogram, token-level log-likelihoods if available.

design-bot: Console notes: need a dedicated Rollback panel under "Deployments" with: current canary % by-tenant, last rollback reason, quick revert button (with confirmation + audit comment), and an expandable diagnostics panel. Mockups needed (I'll drop a prototype). :art: :spiral_notepad: 

Ava Chen: Next steps I propose: 1) Priya to synth PRD skeleton with ACs above and edge cases; 2) Samir to scope infra work and effort estimate; 3) Design to produce 1 mock; 4) Sync w/ customer success to get labeled repro prompts. Target: draft PRD in 1 week.

Priya Nair: I'll create the PRD draft and include an appendix with the customer repro set and suggested eval thresholds.

Samir Shah: I'll run a quick infra feasibility check and post estimates by EOD tomorrow.

Ava Chen: Thanks all — please add short comments to the draft when it's up. :thumbsup:

Need to capture goals, non-goals, and concrete success metrics for a PRD so infra/product can scope rollout controls. @Samir Shah @Priya Nair can you jump in? TL;DR proposal: per-tenant canary + auto-rollback on eval regressions + manual override in console.

Samir Shah: Thanks—quick Q: do we have a repro prompt set from the customer or is this flaky across many prompts? Also what was the traffic % for the canary?

Ava Chen: They shared ~120 failure examples but it's noisy. Canary was 5% traffic for 2h, and errors spiked after a model compact/quantize push. We should treat this as a plausible production regression vector.

Priya Nair: Proposed goals / non-goals (first pass):
- Goals: detect quality regressions per-tenant during rollout, auto-rollback if regression exceeds threshold, surface clear root-cause (model variant + quant config), minimal user intervention for obvious regressions.
- Non-goals: deterministic model debugging (we won't attempt retraining), global-wide rollback policies (this is per-tenant first).
- Success metrics: detection precision >= 95% on labeled regression set; false positive rollback rate < 1% per week; mean rollback time < 45s; customer SLA impact <= 0.5% latency increase.

Liam O'Connor: A couple of edge cases worth listing:
- Intermittent regressions that correlate with specific prompt lengths (need sequence-bucketed evals).
- Tenant-level small sample sizes (5% traffic * small tenant => noisy stats). Mitigation: require sustained drop over window or combine with offline evaluation.

Priya Nair: Example acceptance criteria snippet (for the PRD):
```
AC-1: For any tenant-grade canary, if model variant shows >=3% absolute drop in quality score on the tenant's eval-suite sustained for 3 consecutive minutes, system triggers auto-rollback.
AC-2: Rollback should complete and route traffic back within <= 60s, and emit a rollback event with diagnostics (delta metrics, sample diffs, model id).
AC-3: Manual override button in Console must be available to operators and preserve audit logs.
```

Ava Chen: Nice. Can we tighten the rollback latency target to <=30s if infra says it's feasible? We need that for high-frequency services. @Samir any infra constraints?

Samir Shah: 30s is ambitious but doable if we prioritize the rollback path: pre-warm fallback replicas, keep kv cache primed, and avoid full cold-starts. We need autoscale playbook updates. Also implementing tenant-aware evaluation hooks will need a small infra spike (~2 sprint effort).

Liam O'Connor: Metrics specifics I'd add:
- Primary quality metric: per-tenant EVAL-PASS-RATE (binary per prompt) aggregated as rolling 1-min and 5-min windows.
- Alerting: trigger when 1-min drop >3% AND 5-min drop >1.5%.
- Diagnostic payload: sample diffs (orig vs variant), latency histogram, token-level log-likelihoods if available.

design-bot: Console notes: need a dedicated Rollback panel under "Deployments" with: current canary % by-tenant, last rollback reason, quick revert button (with confirmation + audit comment), and an expandable diagnostics panel. Mockups needed (I'll drop a prototype). :art: :spiral_notepad: 

Ava Chen: Next steps I propose: 1) Priya to synth PRD skeleton with ACs above and edge cases; 2) Samir to scope infra work and effort estimate; 3) Design to produce 1 mock; 4) Sync w/ customer success to get labeled repro prompts. Target: draft PRD in 1 week.

Priya Nair: I'll create the PRD draft and include an appendix with the customer repro set and suggested eval thresholds.

Samir Shah: I'll run a quick infra feasibility check and post estimates by EOD tomorrow.

Ava Chen: Thanks all — please add short comments to the draft when it's up. :thumbsup:

title: #product: 2048001234
preview:
Ava Chen: Quick sync — we need to align on scope & launch criteria for the next observability push: dashboards, tracing hooks, per-tenant cost breakdown, error analytics. Customer asks (AcmeHealth + two mid-market pilots) are pushing for cost-by-route and span-level annotations.
Liam O'Connor: +1. we should be explicit about sampling and retention tradeoffs. full trace retention for every request will blow costs.
Priya Sharma: Agree. Suggested approach: default adaptive sampling + an opt-in full-trace bucket for enterprise customers.
Mateo Ruiz: From SEs: AcmeHealth needs per-tenant cost in billing UI and a CSV export. They said seeing token-level cost by customer is a must for procurement.
Ava Chen: Good note. Proposal MVP: 1) dashboard templates (latency, error rate, cost summary) 2) tracing hooks SDK (span annotations + service tags) 3) cost-by-tenant in console w/ CSV export 4) outgoing alert hooks for error spikes. Launch bar: must satisfy the two pilot customers.
Liam O'Connor: For tracing hooks, plan: small SDK (python/node), server-side ingest that adds span metadata, and a sampling policy service. Implementation risk: ingestion QPS burst handling.
Priya Sharma: Operational constraints: retention defaults -> 7 days for full traces, 30 days for aggregates. If enterprise requests longer we provide dedicated storage via Dedicated customers or S3 export.
Ava Chen: Launch criteria checklist (draft):
- Feature completeness: dashboard templates + trace hook endpoints + tenant-cost export (CSV)
- Performance: 95th perc page load < 500ms for console dashboards with default window
- Cost: ROI check — expected telemetry OPEX increase < 12% for hosted customers at median workload
- Security/compliance: KMS keys for tenant export + audit logs enabled
- Pilot sign-off: AcmeHealth + PilotB acceptability test pass
Liam O'Connor: I'll add acceptance tests. Quick example for the feature flag we'll use:
```
FEATURE_FLAGS = {
  'obs_ui_rollout_v1': true,
  'trace_hook_sdk_beta': false
}
```
Priya Sharma: Ops runbook: include steps to backfill metrics if sampling config changes, and a throttling knob for trace ingestion. Put emergency revert play: toggle `obs_ui_rollout_v1` off.
Mateo Ruiz: Timeline ask from sales: pilot in 3 weeks, general availability in 10 weeks. Is that doable?
Liam O'Connor: 10 weeks feels tight but doable if we scope MVP strictly. We should split into two phases: Phase A (dashboards + cost CSV + simple trace tags) Phase B (SDKs, advanced span-level analytics, exports).
Ava Chen: Agreed. We'll aim Phase A for the 10-week GA. Phase B slips to T+16. Need clarity on what counts for GA.
Priya Sharma: For Phase A GA, require: sampling policy in place, basic trace tag ingest, per-tenant cost card, and runbook. No SDKs required for GA.
Mateo Ruiz: Sounds negotiable with customers. I'll push for the CSV export to be in Phase A as contract language.
Ava Chen: Action items: @Liam add acceptance tests + perf targets to the RFC, @Priya write runbook skeleton, @Mateo get pilot sign-off criteria in writing from AcmeHealth. I'll draft the roadmap card and post link.
telemetry-bot: New doc created: https://redwood.internal/docs/observability/roadmap-phase-a (auto-generated) :page_facing_up:
Liam O'Connor: I'll also add a quick POC for trace ingestion throughput numbers next sprint.
Priya Sharma: +1. Also recommend we add a cost-estimation tool for customers to preview telemetry delta before enabling full traces.
Ava Chen: Good call — add to backlog, label as "preflight".
Mateo Ruiz: Anything else blocking sign-off from product?
Ava Chen: Need cost model numbers and pilot acceptance criteria written. If those land by EOD Friday we'll do a decision review next Monday.
Liam O'Connor: I'll try to get the perf/cost sketch by Thursday.
Priya Sharma: I'll have the runbook draft by Friday.
Mateo Ruiz: I'll confirm AcmeHealth acceptance criteria and post here.
Ava Chen: Perfect, thanks all. :rocket:

title: #product: 1966667000
preview:
Olivia Chen: Heads-up 1: quick review of the compact catalog rows — we're adding a density toggle and the performance badges + version pin behaviors need final UX/a11y sign-off. Figma: https://www.figma.com/file/abcd1234 (compact-rows)
Ravi Menon: updated the compact variant this morning. Two problems I hit: 1) pin icon overlaps the badge on narrow widths, 2) when a version is pinned the row expands and pushes other rows down.
Zoe Kim: on keyboard nav the expanded metadata gets focused before the pin control which is confusing. In compact mode we should keep a single tab stop per row unless the user expands deliberately.
Priya Singh: Accessibility: badges currently announce as "badge" to screen readers. We need a semantic readout: metric name, value, unit, and pass/fail status. Example aria-label: "Throughput 120 rps; p95 latency 18 ms; meets SLO."
Ethan Park: visual layout issue: badges are fetched after the row and cause a content shift. On slow networks you see the label jump. Can we reserve the space with a fixed-width placeholder?
Lina Torres: suggest small placeholder skeleton for badges and reserve width via CSS. That will also make animation less janky.
Ravi Menon: +1 skeleton. Also experimenting with an inline "pinned" pill instead of a full metadata expansion. Keeps row height consistent in compact mode.
Olivia Chen: I like the inline pill idea. Can the pill include a tiny caret to toggle the expanded metadata panel if someone wants more details?
Zoe Kim: yes but we should avoid making the pill a separate focus target unless it's actionable. Prefer: pill visible, single tab to select row, then press Enter or a context menu to expand details.
Priya Singh: aria considerations: if the pill toggles content, it must have aria-expanded and an accessible name. If we keep expansion via row action, make sure focus returns to a sensible control after closing.
Ethan Park: copy question for badge tooltip: current text "Latency: 18ms - OK" feels non-specific. Propose "P95 18ms 1 within SLO" or just "P95 18ms" for brevity.
Olivia Chen: for sighted users short tooltip is fine; but screen readers should get the full context via aria-label.
Ravi Menon: implementation notes: I'll add CSS rule to reserve 80px for badge, skeleton fallback, and a variant with inline pill. Also will increase pin hit target to 32px square for keyboard/touch.
Zoe Kim: i'll update keyboard order so row-level actions come first, then optional inline pill, then any inline controls. I'll run local VO + NVDA checks after PR.
Priya Singh: i'll draft a small a11y acceptance checklist: 1) badge announces full sentence, 2) pinned state is discernible to screen readers, 3) focus order consistent, 4) no layout shifts when badges load.
Lina Torres: QA: can we add a visual regression snapshot for compact rows + density toggle? I can add a Percy snapshot scenario with slow network.
Olivia Chen: timeline: Raj is out, so aim for PR by tomorrow EOD, validation Monday, ship Tuesday if all green. Any blockers?
Ravi Menon: none from engineering other than time, will post Figma variants by EOD.
Zoe Kim: will open PR and include keyboard/focus fixes.
Priya Singh: i'll review the PR for a11y and run manual VO checks after merge.
Olivia Chen: tiny copy task for me: change tooltip to "P95 18ms 1 within SLO" and keep the detailed aria-label for screen readers. thumbs? :thumbsup:
All: :thumbsup:

title: #product: 1955554321
preview:
Olivia Chen: Quick design review — compare table in compact mode + performance badges. Need accessibility + copy checks before we ship the density option. Figma: https://www.figma.com/file/xxxxx (compact-compare frames)
Raj Patel: pushed an update to the compact frames. pin icon is smaller, but it now sits very close to the badge. feels cramped.
Zoe Kim: noticed keyboard focus order is wrong in dense rows — you can tab into the row but the pin button gets skipped.
Priya Singh: accessibility note — VoiceOver reads the badge as just 'badge'. We should expose a descriptive aria-label with metric + unit + intent, e.g. 'Performance: 120 requests per second, p95 12 milliseconds'.
Dan Rivera: also seeing layout shift when badges render asynchronously — on low bandwidth the badge loads after text and pushes content. causes a janky experience.
Emma Liu: can we use a fixed-width skeleton for badges to avoid layout shifts? even a placeholder dot of the same size would help.
Raj Patel: agreed on skeleton. Copy question: current tooltip says 'Latency: 12ms — Good'. that's vague. propose 'P95 latency 12ms — within SLAs'. shorter alt: 'P95 12ms'.
Priya Singh: for screen readers I'd prefer a full sentence in aria-label and a short label for sighted users. example suggestion (aria):
```aria-label="Performance: 120 requests per second; p95 latency 12 milliseconds; meets SLO."```
Zoe Kim: implementation note — we'll wire aria-label to the badge element and keep the visible tooltip for sighted users. need to check focusable order for keyboard users (badge shouldn't steal tab unless actionable).
Mateo Alvarez: one more UX nit: when you pin a version the row height grows due to an added row of metadata. in compact mode that collapses nearby rows visually. maybe show a small inline pill instead of full row expand.
Olivia Chen: good point — prefer inline pill. can we mock that in Figma and test with real data?
Raj Patel: i'll add a variant: inline pill + compact spacing. will also try a slightly bigger hit target for pin so it's keyboard friendly despite density.
Priya Singh: please also add aria-hidden on purely decorative badges and ensure role="img" with alt text where content conveys meaning. I can draft a short acceptance checklist for a11y.
Dan Rivera: will add CSS to reserve space for badge (width: 84px) and implement lazy placeholder. Zoe, can you coordinate the focus order fix in the same PR?
Zoe Kim: yep, i'll take the PR. quick question on testing — do we have a runner for VO smoke tests on the catalog pages?
Emma Liu: not automated yet. I can run manual voiceover checks and dump notes in the Figma comment.
Olivia Chen: timeline? want this ready for rollout on Tuesday's deploy.
Raj Patel: i'll upload updated frames by EOD today.
Zoe Kim: aim to open a PR by tomorrow morning.
Priya Singh: i'll produce the a11y checklist and run a quick validation after the PR lands.

title: #product: 1951234567
preview:
Maya Chen (PM): Quick sync — proposing a lightweight set of onboarding + lifecycle gates for the catalog. Goal: reduce customer surprises and shorten time-to-serve. TL;DR: acceptance tests, perf thresholds, rollout gates, notice windows, fallback variants, and a 30d \"gracepath\" for deprecated models. Thoughts?

Devon Li (Eng): +1. Do we mean lab benchmarks or production e2e? We should separate bench vs prod SLOs.

Priya Shah (ML Eng): My take: two tiered thresholds: bench (evals + unit perf) and production (latency p95, cost per tok, regression on eval suite). Bench pass required to be listed; prod pass required for 1st-week canary.

Ben Carter (Product Eng): Need customer impact matrix for breaking changes. Suggest categories: Minor (quantization change, same API), Major (behavioral regressions), Shutdown (sunset). Each ticket has required comms and migration paths.

Omar Ruiz (Support): Customer ops ask: notify customers 14 days before a pin/sunset for Dedicated; provide a compatibility shim for on-prem if possible.

Maya Chen (PM): Draft acceptance criteria: 

```json
{
  "bench": {
    "accuracy_delta": "<=1% absolute",
    "eval_suite": "customer-weighted",
    "inference_cost": "<=+10% baseline"
  },
  "prod": {
    "p95_latency_ms": "<=300",
    "error_rate": "<0.5%",
    "regression_alerts": "auto-trigger"
  }
}
```

Devon Li (Eng): Agree. Also add "kv-cache" warmup rule for canaries. Canary should run 10k requests or 48h whichever comes first.

Priya Shah (ML Eng): + add synthetic prompts to catch hallucination regressions. We'll attach test set.

Ben Carter (Product Eng): Rollout gates: canary -> 10% -> 50% -> 100% with automatic rollback if quality regresses > threshold. Rollout window should be >=24h at each step.

Maya Chen (PM): For deprecations: propose 30d notice + 2x migration helpers (embeddings re-map script, tokenizer agnostic shim). For "gracepath" customers can request extended support (30/90-day).

Omar Ruiz (Support): Can we surface affected customers via analytics? Need queryable list.

Devon Li (Eng): We'll add tagging on catalog entries: `deprecation_window`, `migration_scripts`, `compat_level`. Example config:

```yaml
catalog_entry:
  id: open-llm-1
  compat_level: backward
  deprecation_window_days: 30
  migration_script: s3://shims/open-llm-1/to-open-llm-2.py
```

Maya Chen (PM): Decision items for next week: 1) decide bench thresholds, 2) confirm canary size/duration, 3) finalize comm windows. Who owns each?

Devon Li (Eng): I'll own canary size/duration. Priya will own bench thresholds. Ben will own comms & customer impact matrix. Omar will own support playbook.

Priya Shah (ML Eng): I'll post the eval sets by EOD Thu.

Ben Carter (Product Eng): I'll draft the comm templates and escalation flows.

Maya Chen (PM): Thanks. @ops-bot can you create a recurring task in #product-runbooks for "catalog-lifecycle" with these owners?

ops-bot: Created task: 'catalog-lifecycle: bench thresholds, canary policy, comms' due 7 days. Assignees: Devon Li, Priya Shah, Ben Carter, Omar Ruiz.

Omar Ruiz (Support): One more ask — let's add a "customer opt-out" flag so customers can freeze model changes for 90 days.

Devon Li (Eng): We'll add `customer_hold` option on Dedicated. Implementation impact minimal.

Maya Chen (PM): Great. Closing notes: aim to ship docs + runbook before next OKR check-in. If anyone hits blockers ping me. :raised_hands:

title: #product: 1854321000
preview:
Aisha Khan: quick design Q — invoice download affordance on the compact spend canvas. thinking about org/project toggle + where the download CTA lives. screenshot: https://redwood.app/ux/shot/1854321000.png
Ben Carter: my instinct: keep primary CTA at the row. accountants expect per-invoice export right where the line is. toolbar-level download felt ambiguous to me. :thinking_face:
Eva Li: Accessibility point: if we keep a row button, it needs a clear aria-label and an accessible name with scope (org/project) and billing period. also ensure keyboard target meets size guidelines.
Samir Patel: Implementation note — per-row buttons blow up the DOM in the virtualized list. We can make a delegated action (one listener, data-row-id) to avoid hydration/SSR issues. Perf matters here.
Aisha Khan: What if we do a hybrid pattern: row-level inline affordance (minimal icon) + a sticky selection toolbar that appears when one or more rows are selected with "Download invoices (selected)" and "Download all (filtered)"? reduces per-row visual weight and keeps discoverability.
Ben Carter: hybrid makes sense. Primary for single-download near row, secondary bulk on toolbar. Also prefer explicit format label e.g., "Download invoice (PDF)" rather than generic "Download".
Eva Li: token breakdown: inline expand vs popover question. Popover keeps vertical density but complicates keyboard and screen reader focus. Inline expand is simpler but increases scroll length.
Samir Patel: both are doable. If inline expand, lazy-load the contents to avoid initial render cost. If popover, ensure focus trap and return focus on close.
Aisha Khan: Good call. Accessibility requirement for popover: on open announce "Tokens for <scope> — estimated cost" via aria-live or role=dialog heading.
ProductBot: AB test scheduled: /ab-test create "invoice-download-experiment" variant=A_row_primary variant=B_toolbar_primary link: https://redwood.app/experiments/32
Eva Li: Microcopy suggestions: toolbar CTA -> "Download invoices (selected)"; row CTA -> "Download invoice (PDF)". Include file type. Consider adding visually-hidden text with project/org context: "Invoice for Acme Corp, project: Payments".
Ben Carter: small ask: show file size estimate? Might be noisy. maybe skip for MVP.
Samir Patel: ARIA example for row button: ```html
<button aria-label="Download invoice for Acme Corp — March 2025 (PDF)" data-row-id="r-123">Download</button>
```
Aisha Khan: Love that. Also add sr-only text that includes billing scope and invoice id for screen reader users. Keep visible label short.
Eva Li: I'll produce updated frames with both patterns (hybrid + toolbar-primary) and microcopy variants. Posting by EOD. :sparkles:
Ben Carter: Acceptance criteria for hybrid: 1) keyboard navigable; 2) screen reader announces scope on open/download; 3) virtualization perf unchanged (95th percentile render within baseline).
Samir Patel: I'll prototype with delegated click and requestIdleCallback for loading token breakdowns to avoid jank.
Aisha Khan: Thanks — I'll update Figma and link the flows here when ready. Tagging @Eva for copy review.
ProductBot: Reminder: AB test will run for 7 days and collect download events, keyboard interactions, and engagement metrics.

title: #product: 1834567890
preview:
Maya: Quick design review — I ran through the new key creation stepper in the console. Step 2 (permissions) is easy to skip because it's visually the same as metadata. users are missing the scope toggles. Sam: Saw that in user testing too. They hit Next then complain the key can't do X. Can we make the scopes section more prominent? maybe a bordered box + short hint 'Who can use this key?' Olivia: Accessibility note: the 'this key will not be shown again' text is not read by screen readers. It's plain text below the input — add aria-describedby on the token field and make the notice aria-live=polite when created. Jordan: Copy question — should the primary CTA be 'Create key' or 'Create and copy key'? My vote: explicit 'Create key' then show the token block with a clear Copy button. auto-copy feels surprising. Maya: +1 for not auto-copying. Also the token display should have a short TTL reveal — mask by default and only show when user clicks Reveal; then hide after 30s. mention that in the copy. Sam: Revoke flow: people accidentally hit revoke during experiments. Add an undo snackbar for 10s after revoke. Also consider adding a soft-revoke state for test projects so production keys don't get nuked by mistake. Olivia: Rotate modal UX: current steps are Generate -> Update apps -> Revoke old. Testers expected rotate to revoke old automatically after they confirmed 'done updating'. We should explicitly surface an 'Overlap period (minutes/hours/days)' control. Jordan: A11y nit: the stepper is implemented as a set of buttons but lacks aria-current on the active step — screen readers announce them as clickable without indicating progress. Add aria-current and role="progressbar" with value now/total. Ben: Suggestion for revoke modal copy: instead of generic 'Are you sure?' try 'This will immediately stop all traffic using this key. You can undo for 10s.' makes consequences clearer and reduces panic. Maya: Empty state: when no keys exist show a sample snippet (monospace) and a single primary CTA 'Create key' plus a secondary 'How keys & scopes work' link. Also show suggested presets: Read-only, Embeddings-only, Full-access. Sam: UI pattern: long scope lists are overwhelming. Use chips with quick presets and a 'More...' accordion for advanced scopes. that should cut cognitive load. Olivia: Small copy tweak: change header 'Scopes' to 'Permissions (Scopes)' — testers understood that better. Jordan: Backend/FE sync: rotate API returns new key only once — front-end must not re-call endpoints that would fail. Add test to ensure the token is surfaced exactly once. Ben: Layout nit — on narrow screens the token block wraps awkwardly. Use a monospace horizontal scroll container instead of wrapping to multiple lines. Maya: Security: show an 'Issued by' badge (project/org) near each key so users know billing/ownership. People confuse service account keys and user keys. Sam: Shortcut idea: add a small 'download .env' with the token inserted for common runtimes (node, python, ruby) after create. saves a step for developers. Olivia: Another a11y thing: rotate modal's confirm button is visually primary but not focusable in DOM order — keyboard users have to tab through the whole list first. Ensure primary CTA is the last focus in the modal and gets initial focus when appropriate. Jordan: QA checklist I'm adding to PR template: aria-current for steppers, aria-describedby on token notice, keyboard focus order, visible undo snackbar for revoke. deploy-bot: Deployed branch ux/key-stepper-a11y to staging @ 2024-11-02T14:23Z :rocket: Ben: I'll run through staging tomorrow morning and jot down any visual tweaks. Maya: I'll update the Figma with permission-chip patterns + copy variants (three CTA options). Will DM the copy list. :thumbsup: Sam: thanks — this feels like a good set of changes. Jordan: tagging @Olivia to help with aria wording examples. Olivia: happy to — I'll add exact aria attributes and sample HTML snippets in the PR comment. Ben: quick example snippet I used for copy button:```html
••••••••••••••••
``` Maya: perfect. I'll close this thread once Figma + copy landed. thanks all!

Ava Chen: quick design check — iterating on the playground chat UI. main goals: streaming display clarity, a single stop/regenerate affordance, token counter placement, and prompt-library preview. dropping a figma link in a sec. thoughts? Jasper: +1 on consolidating stop/regenerate. do we show both states as one button with label swap, or two separate actions? Elaine M: prefer one dynamic primary button. when streaming -> label `Stop`, after finish -> `Regenerate`. reduces visual clutter for keyboard users. Tom R: accessibility: ensure the streaming area uses `role=status`/`aria-live` so screen readers hear token arrival. also keyboard support for Stop (Enter) while focused. Riya Patel: tiny copy nit: on mobile `Stop` can be ambiguous. propose `Stop generation` for small widths, desktop stays `Stop`. Jasper: re token counter — where to surface it? inline in the input, floating top-right, or inside the prompt library preview? Elaine M: my vote: compact counter inside the input area (right side) and a more detailed hover/expand in the prompt drawer. keeps context near typing. Tom R: +1. counter must be accessible too (aria-label e.g., `Token usage: 142 tokens`). avoid purely visual meter. Ava Chen: rough accessibility snippet for the stream: ```
Streaming text…
142 ``` Riya Patel: also suggest a focus ring on the Stop button that's more visible than the default — it's a critical action. designbot: deployed preview: https://redwood-internal/figma/playground/stream-ux-2026 (comment thread enabled) Jasper: layout concern — if we put token counter inside input on very small screens it can push send button. Maybe collapse to an icon that expands on tap. Elaine M: yep, icon + tooltip on tap is good. tooltip should include both tokens and approximate cost estimate (rounded). Tom R: small CSS idea for token chip: ` .token-chip { font-size:12px; opacity:0.7; padding:2px 6px; } ` keeps it subtle. Ava Chen: decision proposal: 1) single dynamic primary button (Stop -> Regenerate) 2) token counter as compact chip in input, expands in prompt drawer 3) aria-live + role=status for stream 4) copy change on mobile to `Stop generation` 5) mock + a11y pass next week. Riya Patel: :thumbsup: i'll update copy and prepare a short test doc for mobile phrasing. Jasper: i'll push mocks by EOD Monday and include a compact/expanded state and keyboard flows. Elaine M: i'll run axe + NVDA checks on the prototype and list any focus-order issues. Tom R: i'll add aria attributes and keyboard handlers in the front-end PR. Ava Chen: sweet — action items captured. tagging follow-ups: @Jasper @Elaine M @Tom R @Riya Patel. thanks all :sparkles: Jasper: quick Q — should Regenerate keep previous tokens visible or collapse? leaning to keep them but greyed, so users can compare. Elaine M: keep but visually de-emphasize, also expose a dismiss action. Ava Chen: agreed. that'll be in the mock. closing here, will open a PR with final copy + design spec when mocks land.

title: #product: 1809998887
preview:
Leah (PM): Quick sync — thinking we should formalize 'platform ladders' as the prioritization + acceptance rubric for routing/multi-region features. Keep convo short: what tiers, gating metrics, and customer ask mapping?
Ava Chen: +1. Proposal: three ladders — Pilot (beta customers), Scale (dedicated customers), Harden (SLA + enterprise). Each needs clear latency p50/p95, cost delta, and isolation guarantees.
Mason Patel: Agree. For latency: Pilot p95 <= 250ms, Scale p95 <= 180ms, Harden p95 <= 120ms. Cost: relative token cost delta vs baseline < 25/15/5% respectively.
Priya Singh: For isolation, Pilot = soft tenant fencing (logical), Scale = tenant pools + quotas, Harden = dedicated slices + region pinning. Also need evict policy and runbooks.
Leah (PM): Good. Customer asks: fintech/healthcare require Harden. Start mapping active customers to ladders and call out migration runway.
Sam Ortiz: Ops note — Harden requires KMS + audit logs and cross-region DR. Estimated infra work: 6w for provisioning APIs + 4w for runbooks.
Ava Chen: Can we add automated canary metrics for quality: prompt evals pass rate >= 98% and regressions <= 0.5% on rollout?
Mason Patel: Yes. Also add KB cache hit targets: Pilot 20%, Scale 40%, Harden 60% for typical chat workloads.
Priya Singh: Dependencies: routing policy DSL, tenant affinity metadata, autoscaler hooks, billing tagging. Prioritize DSL + affinity first.
Leah (PM): Action items: 1) Engineering to draft acceptance checklist doc (Ava + Mason) due EOD Friday. 2) Product to map customers to ladders (Leah) due next Tues. 3) Ops to estimate infra cost (Sam) due Wed.

title: #product: 1799998000
preview:
Ava Chen: quick design flag — when org has SSO enforced, the invite progress toast still shows a generic 'Invite sent' state. users expect an explicit 'SSO enforced — link sent to IdP' or similar. also the toast disappears too fast :thinking_face:
Marcus Lee: +1 saw that. repro: invite a user while SSO enforced on the org, the invite flow redirects to the idp, but the toast doesn't mention SSO. can we surface an affordance to view next steps? :eyes:
Priya Khatri: UX note — if we add extra copy it needs to be short and actionable for keyboard/screen-reader users. suggestion: 'SSO enforced — user must accept via corporate SSO. Open org SSO settings' with an inline link. also color contrast for the SSO badge is low on the current teal background.
Sam Torres: dev note — the current toast component auto-dismiss is 3s. for SSO states I think it should be persistent until dismissed or link clicked. easy change, but impacts analytics.
designbot: Reminder: new a11y audit requires toast ARIA-live politeness = 'assertive' for critical state changes.
Ava Chen: good call on ARIA. Marcus / Sam — can we get a variant: persistent toast + 'Open SSO settings' CTA; copy to be 1 line max. Priya can you propose exact wording?
Priya Khatri: proposed copy: 'SSO required — user must sign in via your IdP. Open SSO settings.' fallback shorter: 'SSO required — open settings.' also propose tooltip on the SSO toggle: 'Enforced: members must authenticate via your IdP.'
Marcus Lee: technical caveat — when invites are sent via API (bulk invites) there's a different success event stream. toasts there are batched; making them persistent for SSO will require a different UX (per-invite vs aggregated). maybe show an aggregated banner at top of the workspace in that case.
Sam Torres: ack. alternate: toast for single invites persistent; for bulk show top-banner with count + 'Review SSO invite states' CTA. Dev work estimation ~2d to implement toast variant + banner route.
Ava Chen: accessibility nit — ensure toast focus isn't stolen if user is typing in the invite field. we should move focus to the toast only if user hits 'Open SSO settings'. otherwise announce via live region only.
Priya Khatri: also audit log navigation: when user clicks 'Review SSO invite states' we currently land on Audit -> Events but the filter for 'invite' isn't applied. need deep-link params: ?event=invite&type=sso so they see the right rows. otherwise they get lost.
Marcus Lee: agreed. backend already emits event_type=invite and meta.sso=true. Sam can you confirm UI supports that query param?
Sam Torres: UI supports filters in querystring but audit table columns load lazily, so we need to ensure the filter is applied during initial render to avoid flicker. small change.
Ava Chen: final checklist for PR: 1) persistent SSO toast variant + CTA 2) updated copy from Priya 3) ARIA-live assertive + no focus steal 4) color contrast tweak for SSO badge 5) bulk-invite banner path 6) deep-link params to audit log. I'll draft a quick spec and attach screenshots in next message.
Priya Khatri: thanks — I'll post microcopy options and color token suggestions in #design later. :+1:
Marcus Lee: will slot a 30m review with eng tomorrow. Sam — can you rough-timeline the frontend/back-end pieces?
Sam Torres: frontend ~2d. backend for audit deep-link trivial, 0.5d. will follow up in the review.
Ava Chen: awesome, thanks team. tiny follow-up: for analytics, track 'sso_toast_opened', 'sso_toast_dismissed', 'audit_deep_link_clicked'.
Designbot: Note: added a11y checklist item — 'toast should be reachable via keyboard after open' to the ticket.

title: #product: 1798765432
preview:
Ava Chen: Quick sync — I prototyped a flow where min/max are inline steppers and burst is a compact pill. Noticed tab order jumps when toggling chain icon. Thoughts?
Samir Patel: can you attach the prototype link? also looks fine on wide screens but mobile stack might be weird.
Ava Chen: https://figma.com/proto/xyz (mock) — try the keyboard flow: Tab -> min stepper -> max stepper -> chain toggle -> burst pill.
Tomás Ruiz: Reproduced. When chain is ON, focus moves from max -> chain -> burst. When OFF, chain is visually present but removed from tab order which causes focus to skip back to top of the form. Feels jarring.
Priya Nair: Suggestion: keep chain toggle in tab order but mark aria-pressed="true/false" and aria-describedby to include the linked behavior. That avoids focus jumps and maintains semantics.
QA Bot: a11y check: burst pill color contrast (disabled state) is 2.7:1 — below AA. Also stepper +/- hit targets are 28px on desktop which is OK but on touch it's borderline.
DesignBot: microcopy tweak: instead of "Burst" label consider "Burst quota" or add tooltip: "Burst quota — temporary extra capacity charged per-second."
Samir Patel: UX question: should burst consumption show a small progress bar inside the pill? might make it clearer how much of monthly burst is left.
Ava Chen: I like progress inside pill, but small — we should avoid adding heavy visuals that shift layout. Maybe a 2px bar under the pill.
Tomás Ruiz: Keyboard behavior for SLO slider also needs attention. Current tabindex order puts slider before min/max. Users expect capacity knobs first.
Priya Nair: Agree. Proposed order: Pool selector -> min stepper -> max stepper -> chain toggle -> burst pill -> SLO slider -> advanced link.
Ava Chen: I'll update prototype. Also adding explicit focus outlines: use 3px outline-offset and theme-aware color so focus remains visible on dark and light.
QA Bot: Flag: aria-live region for live cost preview currently announces updates for every token change (spam). Should debounce to 500ms and announce "Estimated cost updated" with new value.
DesignBot: Copy nits on SLO slider: current label "SLO (latency)" -> prefer "Latency SLO (p99 ms)" and tooltip: "Target tail latency for requests; affects routing and fallback policies."
Samir Patel: Mobile: pool picker uses a drop-in that covers full screen. If we pin burst pill to top-right it overlaps the picker header. Need z-index plan.
Ava Chen: Action items: 1) Ava — update figma: change tab order & keep chain toggle tabbable (due EOD). 2) Priya — write aria-describedby content for chain/ burst (tomorrow). 3) Tomás — a11y audit on contrast + touch sizes (this week). 4) Samir — mobile z-index check + pill placement (next sprint).
Priya Nair: Also propose copy for confirmation modal when lowering max below current running jobs: "Lowering max may evict running jobs. Confirm to proceed or pick a higher max."
Tomás Ruiz: +1 on modal copy. Add inline link to eviction docs.
Ava Chen: Thanks all — I'll post updated prototype and screenshot when done. :rocket:
QA Bot: Reminder: run axe with the new build before user testing.
Samir Patel: sounds good. thumbs up :thumbsup:

preview:
Sofia Alvarez: quick pass on the compact routing editor mock — focus areas: reorder affordance, validation visibility, and sim preview noise. figma: https://www.figma.com/file/compact-ux

Ben Carter: looks good overall. two quick nits: the drag handle hit area feels tiny and the reorder animation causes the preview snapshot to flicker. could we make the handle full-row height and animate transforms only? :eyes:

title: #product: 1778889000
preview:
Marta Ruiz: Short recap from today's routing-patterns workshop. We focused on making a productized set of 'residency dials' for customers: how to express residency/latency/cost preferences and what we will guarantee. Keep replies concise and nominate owners.
Liam O'Connor: Love the dials metaphor. Proposal: three core templates (latency-priority, cost-shared, residency-strong). Each has a fallback behavior and a pricing band.
Priya Nair: Implementation reality check: templates must map to concrete routing rules + scheduler hints + model-variant pins (e.g., int8 vs fp16). Document a sample mapping per template.
Ethan Park: Ops ask: define headroom and evacuation signals per region. If headroom < 7% trigger evacuation flow. Need a clear default: soft residency -> spill; hard residency -> reject + page.
Sofia Gomez: Sales input: customers want expected lead time and a one-line description for collateral. I'll craft a short blurb per template and rough lead times.
Nora Blake: Legal: any "hard residency" must include auditability and a minimum term. Flag for EU data residency wording and KMS requirements.
Marta Ruiz: Draft minimal launch checklist (for RFC):
- Canary: template-specific (min 24h, typical 48h at >=10%)
- Telemetry: p50/p95 latency, error rate, token cost delta, cache hit-rate
- Quality: eval suite pass; block on regressions >2% absolute on core prompts
- Capacity: regional headroom >=10% before full cutover; warm KV cache plan
- Compliance: audit log with who/why + KMS check for hard residency
- Runbook: automated rollback + notification hooks and escalation path
Liam O'Connor: Add policy precedence doc: tenant override > region rule > org default. Include examples in DSL so product teams can read.
Priya Nair: Migration YAML sketch for RFC:
```
migration:
  tenant: "acct-123"
  template: "residency-strong"
  canary_pct: "10"
  warm_kv: "true"
  eval_suite: "core-prompts-v3"
  rollback_triggers:
    p95_increase_pct: "20"
    quality_regression_pct: "3"
```
Ethan Park: Evacuation matrix (ops):
- soft residency: overflow -> shared pool (best-effort), queue up to 30s
- hard residency: overflow -> reject (429) + immediate page + support-runbook
- latency-priority: spill to nearest low-latency pool, degrade throughput if needed
Sofia Gomez: Pricing stub suggestions (ballpark): soft residency +12%, latency-priority +6%, hard residency +30%. Finance must validate.
Nora Blake: Contract stub notes: require minimum 60-day commit for hard residency; include audit/export logs clause and KMS escrow option.
Marta Ruiz: Owners: Priya -> migration tooling spike; Liam -> policy DSL + precedence examples; Ethan -> evacuation playbook; Sofia -> sales blurbs & lead times; Nora -> contract language.
ci-bot: RFC created at https://go.redwood/docs/residency-dials-rfc (please review and comment).
Marta Ruiz: Thanks team. I'll collate these snippets into the RFC and schedule a follow-up to resolve pricing and headroom thresholds. Please drop short comments in the RFC rather than long threads.

title: #product: 1772032111
preview:
Chris Osei: Need a decision today on the publication criteria for “Known Issues” so we can lock PM-913 + unblock Docs PRs. Specifically: (1) confidence threshold to call something a known issue vs “investigating”, (2) workaround validation bar, (3) who owns updates/expiry. Draft is in PM-913: https://linear.app/redwood/issue/PM-913

Dev Patel: +1. Support keeps getting stuck in limbo where we *know* it’s real but Engineering doesn’t want us to publish until there’s an RCA. We need a middle ground.

Elliot Price: From docs POV: we can publish without RCA, but we need clear labeling and update responsibilities. Also we need to avoid anything customer-identifying. (We’ve had drafts with account IDs / exact tenant names—those can’t ship.)

Rachel Kim: Enterprise angle: customers don’t care if we have RCA yet, they care if the workaround is safe + the comms are consistent. But we can’t publish “try random knobs” and then eat it later.

Sean Gallagher: SRE request: don’t publish “known issue” unless there’s at least one of:
- confirmed telemetry signature (dashboards/alerts)
- internal repro
- multiple independent customer reports with matching request IDs + symptom pattern
If it’s “one customer says X” it’s not “known”, it’s “investigating”.

Dev Patel: Works. Can we define it as:
A) “Known issue” = we have high confidence it’s a real platform behavior + we have a workaround or mitigation steps that do not make things worse
B) “Investigating” = we’ve seen reports but no confirmed signal/repro
And we publish only A externally.

Priya Natarajan: Platform agrees with publish-only-A. For A, we should require an Eng/SRE reviewer, not just Support.

Owen Phillips: +1 reviewer requirement. We also need to state “When to escalate” explicitly so Support doesn’t keep re-triaging the same thing. That section has been missing in a bunch of drafts.

Wei Chen: Proposal for confidence threshold (so Support isn’t blocked on “RCA”):
- “Known issue” if *any* of:
  1) 3+ tickets in 14 days with same symptom pattern (region/model/tier tagged)
  2) 1 enterprise P0/P1 with Eng/SRE confirmation (telemetry or repro)
  3) internal incident or degradation event where comms already went out
- “Investigating” otherwise (internal-only page or keep in SUP ticket)
Does that satisfy SRE?

Sean Gallagher: Mostly. I’d tweak #1 to “3+ tickets OR 2+ distinct customers” because sometimes one big customer spams multiple tickets.

Jasmine Liu: Also add a fast path: if Support is getting repeat tickets *and* there’s a low-risk workaround that reduces impact, we should publish after reviewer sign-off even if the count is low.

Chris Osei: So: known issue requires confirmation + reviewer sign-off, but count can trigger the “we should go confirm this now” workflow.

Sofia Mendes: Legal/contract language: do not use “SLA breach” wording in KB articles. Use “service degradation” / “elevated errors” / “increased latency”. Also avoid promising timelines (“will be fixed by Friday”). Use “we’re working on a fix” + “next update by …” only in customer comms, not evergreen KB.

Kira Thompson: On workaround validation bar: for Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private it’s different.
- Hosted: workaround must be client-side or request-level; cannot instruct customers to change infra we control.
- Dedicated: config changes allowed, but needs a rollback step + “expected outcome” + “risk”.
- Private: any cluster/installer action must be validated by Private Deployments/Platform; include what logs to collect before/after.

Dev Patel: +1. Can we codify “validated workaround” as:
- tested by Redwood (repro in staging OR at least one verified customer confirmation)
- reviewed by the owning Eng team (SRE/Platform/Runtime)
- includes: steps, expected result, rollback, and “when to escalate”

Priya Natarajan: For Platform-owned issues (SSO/audit logs/quota), reviewer is Platform oncall or named DRI; for routing/fallback, Mina’s team; for runtime perf, runtime oncall.

Sean Gallagher: And for anything that touches incident processes (regional degradation / widespread 5xx), add a line: “If you suspect broad impact, follow incident comms/status page guidance” + link. Otherwise we get docs used as substitute for status page.

Elliot Price: We can implement a template block in the KB:
- Status: Active | Monitoring | Resolved
- Last updated: date
- Affected: regions/models/tiers
- Workaround (validated)
- When to escalate
- Internal notes (NOT published)
And for “Resolved”, we keep it for 30 days then archive.

Rachel Kim: Who owns updates? I need this to not rot. Customers will screenshot stale KB.

Dev Patel: Suggest:
- Support owns the article (primary) + keeps “last updated” current
- Eng reviewer owns technical accuracy + must sign off on any material change
- If there’s a fix shipped, Eng DRI pings Support/Docs to update status + remove workaround if no longer needed

Chris Osei: That maps to a RACI. Also we can set an SLA for *updates*:
- Active known issues: review every 7 days minimum (even if “no change”) and bump last-updated
- Monitoring: review every 14 days
- Resolved: mark resolved + add fix version/date, then archive after 30 days

Sean Gallagher: 7 days is fine as a minimum. If it’s tied to an incident, updates will be more frequent via comms anyway.

Sofia Mendes: One more: include a disclaimer that workarounds are temporary and may vary by environment, esp Dedicated/Private.

Elliot Price: Decision check (so I can comment on PR-2841 review guidance):
1) We only publish external “Known Issues” when confidence is high (telemetry/repro/confirmed pattern) AND there is a validated workaround/mitigation.
2) “Investigating” stays internal (Support-only notes / SUP ticket), not in public KB.
3) Workaround must be reviewed by owning Eng/SRE + include steps, expected outcome, rollback, and “when to escalate”.
4) Ownership: Support DRI maintains; Eng DRI signs off; weekly/biweekly review cadence by status.
If yes, I’ll bake this into the publication checklist + template.

Jasmine Liu: Yes from CS. :thumbsup:

Dev Patel: Yes. This is exactly what Support needs.

Sean Gallagher: Yes with the “2+ distinct customers” tweak noted. :thumbsup:

Priya Natarajan: Yes. Please make sure the reviewer list in the playbook matches real oncall rotations (don’t name a team alias that doesn’t page anyone).

Chris Osei: Locked. I’ll update PM-913 with: “Known issue = high confidence + validated workaround + reviewer sign-off; investigating = internal-only.” Also adding the update cadence + RACI.

Wei Chen: I’ll update the KB template to include “Validated workaround” and “When to escalate” as required headings.
